rejected_candidates.title: Router fee rounding permits per-call fee avoidance on dust-sized trades
rejected_candidates.reason: The floor-division behavior is real but only avoids fees on dust-sized mainnet trades and is economically immaterial; meaningful fee bypass is already captured by F-011.

rejected_candidates.title: LamboToken implementation can be initialized and minted by anyone
rejected_candidates.reason: Initializing the implementation affects only the implementation contract's own storage. Factory-created clones have independent storage and are initialized atomically, so this is an off-chain confusion risk rather than protocol-level harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Rebalancer does not clear external OKX token-approve allowances
rejected_candidates.reason: The concrete in-scope swap path approves the exact input for an exact-input OKX/UniswapV3 swap, so leftover allowance requires speculative external under-consumption or approve-proxy compromise. The code does not support a realistic standalone loss scenario.

rejected_candidates.title: Initialize Can Be Called By Anyone After Deployment
rejected_candidates.reason: The claimed clone initialization race is not possible: LamboFactory deploys the clone and calls initialize in the same transaction before any external caller can interact with the new clone address.

rejected_candidates.title: Factory createLaunchPad Has No Return Value Validation For takeLoan
rejected_candidates.reason: VirtualToken.takeLoan has no return value and either mints the exact requested amount or reverts on the per-block cap. It cannot silently mint a smaller loan amount.

rejected_candidates.title: Router Does Not Validate That Pair Exists Before Swap
rejected_candidates.reason: If the pair does not exist or has no liquidity, getReserves/getAmountOut reverts and the whole transaction rolls back, including the earlier token transfer. This is a revert/UX issue, not fund loss.

rejected_candidates.title: Rebalance Allows Zero Amount In With Flash Loan
rejected_candidates.reason: Zero or near-zero unprofitable calls revert on the profit check, so the protocol does not pay persistent flash-loan costs; the caller bears gas. Arbitrary profitable trade sizing is already captured by F-019.

rejected_candidates.title: VirtualToken cashIn Uses msg.value For Non-Native Underlying
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a duplicate of F-001. The ETH-lock angle is also covered by the same msg.value/accounting bug and by the broader native-ETH recovery issue in F-015.
